comment_text.ud8.4:3.1: This passage is quoted at MN 144:11.3 and SN 35.87:11.3, and referred to at Snp 3.12:44.3.

root_text.ud8.4:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud8.4:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhū nibbānapaṭisaṁyuttāya dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.ud8.4:1.4: Tedha bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.ud8.4:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud8.4:3.6: Cutūpapāte asati nevidha na huraṁ na ubhayamantarena.

translation_text.ud8.4:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud8.4:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk about extinguishment.
translation_text.ud8.4:1.4: And those mendicants were paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.ud8.4:2.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud8.4:3.1: “For the dependent there is agitation. For the independent there’s no agitation.
translation_text.ud8.4:3.2: When there’s no agitation there is tranquility.
translation_text.ud8.4:3.3: When there is tranquility there’s no inclination.
translation_text.ud8.4:3.4: When there’s no inclination, there’s no coming and going.
translation_text.ud8.4:3.5: When there’s no coming and going, there’s no passing away and reappearing.
translation_text.ud8.4:3.6: When there’s no passing away and reappearing there’s no this life or the next or between the two.
translation_text.ud8.4:3.7: Just this is the end of suffering.”
